title: Chicken Burrito Bowls
description: Diced chicken with black beans, corn, and cheddar over jasmine rice.

instructions.text: Cook the rice: rinse 1½ cups jasmine rice, simmer with 2¼ cups water for 18 minutes, then rest 5 minutes off heat. Fluff with a fork.
While rice cooks, season the chicken breasts on both sides with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Bake the chicken on a sheet pan at 400°F for 22–25 minutes until 165°F internal.
Drain and rinse the black beans and corn; warm them together in a small saucepan over low heat.
Rest the chicken 5 minutes, then dice into bite-size pieces.
Build the bowls: rice on the bottom, then diced chicken, beans + corn, shredded cheddar, and a handful of spinach on top.

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Foil pan: diced chicken + black beans + corn mixed. Gallon bag: rice frozen flat. Quart bag: shredded cheddar.
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Spread rice in foil pan, cover, bake 375F 20 min. Reheat chicken-bean pan covered at 375F 25 min. Serve bowl-style with cheddar and spinach on top.

title: Beef Bolognese over Spaghetti
description: Rich meat sauce over spaghetti with parmesan.

instructions.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the spaghetti.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Sweat the diced onion for 5 minutes until translucent, then add the garlic and cook 30 seconds more.
Add the ground beef. Brown for 6–8 minutes, breaking it up with a spoon, until no pink remains. Drain off excess fat.
Stir in diced tomatoes, tomato paste, salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Simmer uncovered on low for 30–40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened.
While the sauce simmers, cook the spaghetti to al dente per package directions (usually 9–11 minutes). Drain and toss with a drizzle of olive oil to keep it loose.
Plate the spaghetti, spoon the bolognese on top, and finish with a heavy hand of grated parmesan.

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Foil pan 1: Bolognese meat sauce. Foil pan 2: cooked spaghetti tossed with EVOO. Quart bag: grated parm.
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Bake sauce pan covered at 375F 25-30 min, stirring halfway. Bake pasta pan covered at 350F 20 min (sprinkle 2 tbsp water over pasta before sealing). Serve sauce over pasta, top with parm.

title: Teriyaki Salmon & Broccoli Rice
description: Glazed salmon fillets with steamed broccoli over jasmine rice.

instructions.text: Thaw the salmon fillets if frozen — under cold running water, about 5–10 minutes.
Cook the rice: rinse 1½ cups jasmine rice, simmer with 2¼ cups water for 18 minutes, then rest 5 minutes off heat.
Heat oven to 400°F. Whisk soy sauce, honey, and minced garlic together in a small bowl — that's the teriyaki glaze.
Pat the salmon dry, brush with half the glaze, and place on a parchment-lined sheet pan. Bake 12–15 minutes until just flaky.
Steam the broccoli: 4–5 minutes in the microwave-safe bag, or 5 minutes in a covered pan with a splash of water.
Plate rice, salmon, and broccoli. Drizzle the remaining glaze over the salmon.

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Foil pan 1: salmon fillets with steamed broccoli alongside. Foil pan 2: jasmine rice. Small jar: teriyaki glaze (fridge, don't freeze).
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Bake rice pan covered at 350F 20 min. Bake salmon-broccoli pan covered at 325F 15-18 min. Drizzle glaze at the table.

title: Chicken & Veggie Stir-Fry
description: Chicken strips with stir-fry vegetables in soy-garlic sauce over rice.

instructions.text: Cook the rice: rinse 1½ cups jasmine rice, simmer with 2¼ cups water for 18 minutes, then rest off heat.
Slice the chicken tenderloins into thin strips against the grain.
Heat 1 tbsp avocado oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat. Stir-fry the chicken with half the garlic for 4–5 minutes until just cooked through. Move to a plate.
Add the second tbsp of oil to the same pan. Stir-fry the frozen vegetable blend for 5–6 minutes until tender-crisp; add the remaining garlic in the last minute.
Return the chicken to the pan. Add soy sauce and toss everything together for 1 minute to coat.
Serve the stir-fry over the rice.

ingredients.display_quantity: 1½ lb (about half of a 3 lb bag)

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Foil pan: chicken strips + stir-fry vegetables mixed. Gallon bag: rice frozen flat. Small container: soy-garlic sauce.
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Spread rice in foil pan, cover, bake 375F 20 min. Reheat stir-fry pan covered at 375F 20-25 min. Toss with sauce or serve on side.

title: Turkey Chili
description: Hearty chili with ground turkey, black beans, tomatoes, and corn.

instructions.text: In a Dutch oven over medium heat, cook the diced onion for 5 minutes until translucent. Add minced garlic for 30 seconds.
Add the ground turkey. Brown for 6–8 minutes, breaking it up, until no pink remains.
Stir in diced tomatoes, tomato paste, drained beans, drained corn, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Simmer uncovered on low for 25–30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened.
Taste and adjust salt. Ladle into bowls and top with shredded cheddar.

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Single foil pan: everything in one. Quart bag: shredded cheddar for topping.
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Bake covered at 375F 30-35 min, stirring once halfway. Top with cheddar at the table.

title: Coconut Shrimp Curry
description: Shrimp in coconut curry sauce with stir-fry vegetables over rice.

instructions.text: Cook the rice: rinse 1½ cups jasmine rice, simmer with 2¼ cups water for 18 minutes, then rest off heat.
Thaw the shrimp under cold running water (5–10 minutes) and pat dry.
Heat coconut oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Sweat the diced onion for 5 minutes, then add garlic for 30 seconds.
Pour in both cans of coconut milk and the soy sauce; bring to a simmer. Add the frozen vegetable blend and cook 5 minutes.
Add the shrimp and salt. Simmer 4–5 more minutes until the shrimp turn pink and curl. Don't overcook.
Spoon the curry over the rice.

ingredients.display_quantity: 1½ lb (about half of a 3 lb bag)

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Foil pan 1: shrimp curry with vegetables in coconut sauce. Foil pan 2: jasmine rice.
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Bake rice pan covered at 350F 20 min. Bake curry pan covered at 350F 20-25 min (don't go higher — coconut milk can break at high heat). Stir gently.

title: Baked Penne Bolognese
description: Penne with Bolognese sauce, spinach, and bubbly mozzarella.

instructions.text: Heat oven to 375°F. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
Brown the ground beef in a large skillet over medium heat, 6–8 minutes; drain. Stir in diced tomatoes, tomato paste, and tomato sauce. Simmer 10 minutes to thicken.
Cook the penne to AL DENTE — about 2 minutes shy of package directions, since it'll bake more. Drain.
Thaw frozen spinach in the microwave (3–4 min on defrost), then squeeze dry in a clean towel.
In a 9×13 baking dish: layer half the pasta, all of the spinach, all of the bolognese, the rest of the pasta, the marinara, and finally all of the mozzarella. Dot the top with butter.
Bake covered with foil for 20 minutes. Uncover and bake 10 more minutes until the cheese is bubbly and golden.

ingredients.display_quantity: 1 lb (one bag), thawed and squeezed dry

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Single foil pan: fully assembled casserole — penne + Bolognese + spinach + tomato sauce + mozzarella on top.
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Bake covered at 375F 30 min, then uncover and bake 10 min more until cheese is bubbly and golden.

title: Chicken Tikka-Style Bowls
description: Tikka-spiced chicken with garbanzo beans in creamy tomato sauce over basmati rice.

instructions.text: Marinate the chicken: cube the breasts and toss with ½ cup yogurt, half the minced garlic, 1 tbsp tomato paste, garlic powder, and salt. Rest 15 minutes (or up to overnight).
Cook the basmati rice: rinse 2 cups, simmer with 3 cups water for 18 minutes, then rest off heat.
Heat oven to 425°F. Spread the marinated chicken on a parchment-lined sheet pan and bake 18–20 minutes until 165°F internal.

While the chicken roasts, make the sauce: sweat onion in a saucepan over medium heat for 5 minutes, add the remaining garlic for 30 seconds, then stir in tomato sauce and the rest of the tomato paste. Simmer 10 minutes.
Off the heat, stir the remaining ¼ cup yogurt into the sauce until smooth. Add the drained garbanzo beans and the roasted chicken.
Serve over basmati rice with a handful of fresh spinach on top.

ingredients.display_quantity: ¾ cup (split: ½ cup for marinade, ¼ cup stirred into sauce)

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Foil pan 1: tikka chicken chunks in sauce + garbanzo beans. Foil pan 2: basmati rice. Fresh spinach day-of.
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Bake rice pan covered at 350F 20 min. Bake tikka pan covered at 375F 25-30 min, stirring once. Serve over rice with fresh spinach.

title: Roasted Pork Tenderloin Plates
description: Sliced pork medallions with roasted sweet potatoes and buttered green beans.

instructions.text: Heat oven to 400°F. Cube sweet potatoes into ¾-inch pieces; toss with avocado oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a sheet pan and start roasting — they'll go for 30 minutes total.
Pat the pork tenderloins dry. Season all over with garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Drizzle with olive oil.
After the sweet potatoes have roasted 10 minutes, slide the pork onto a second sheet pan into the oven. Roast 20–25 minutes until 145°F internal.

While the pork roasts, melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add minced garlic for 30 seconds, then add the frozen green beans straight from the bag. Cover and cook 5 minutes, then uncover and sauté 3 more minutes.
Rest the pork 10 minutes before slicing into ½-inch medallions.
Plate: pork medallions, sweet potatoes, green beans.

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Foil pan 1: sliced pork medallions. Foil pan 2: roasted sweet potatoes and sauteed green beans.
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Bake both pans covered at 350F 20-25 min.

title: Fish Taco Kits
description: Seasoned cod with black beans, corn, and cabbage in flour tortillas.

instructions.text: Take cod fillets out of the freezer to thaw if not already done — 30 minutes on the counter, or 5 minutes under cold running water.
Drain and rinse the black beans and corn. Combine in a small saucepan over low heat to warm through while everything else cooks.
Pat the cod fillets dry with paper towels, then season both sides with garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering, about 1 minute.
Pan-sear the cod 3–4 minutes per side until opaque and flaky. Transfer to a plate and break into large chunks with a fork.
Warm the tortillas: 30 seconds in a dry skillet over medium heat, or wrap in foil and warm in a 300°F oven for 5 minutes.
Assemble: tortilla, cod, spoonful of warm bean-corn mix, handful of baby spinach. Serve immediately.

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Gallon bag 1: seasoned cod flakes + black beans + corn frozen flat. Gallon bag 2: shredded cabbage or spinach (fridge). Tortillas in original packaging.
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw fish-bean bag overnight. Transfer to foil pan, cover, bake 350F 15-20 min. Warm tortillas in dry skillet or foil in oven. Assemble at the table.

title: Peanut Chicken Noodles
description: Spaghetti in creamy peanut sauce with shredded chicken and edamame.

instructions.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the spaghetti.
Poach the chicken: cover the tenderloins with cold water in a saucepan, bring to a bare simmer, cook 12 minutes until 165°F. Cool, then shred with two forks.
Cook the spaghetti to al dente per package directions. Reserve ½ cup pasta water; drain the pasta.
Meanwhile, whisk peanut butter, soy sauce, honey, minced garlic, and ¼ cup warm pasta water in a large bowl until smooth. Thin further with pasta water as needed.
Cook the edamame in the microwave per package (3–4 minutes) and drain.
Toss the hot pasta in the peanut sauce, then fold in the shredded chicken and edamame. Serve immediately, drizzling extra sauce on top if desired.

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Single foil pan: spaghetti + peanut sauce + shredded chicken + edamame all combined. Extra sauce drizzled on top.
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Sprinkle 3-4 tbsp water over top, cover tightly. Bake at 350F 25-30 min. Toss gently before serving.

title: Beef & Black Bean Burritos
description: Seasoned beef and bean filling with rice and cheddar in flour tortillas.

instructions.text: Cook the rice: rinse 1½ cups jasmine rice, simmer with 2¼ cups water for 18 minutes, then rest off heat.
In a large skillet over medium heat, sweat the diced onion for 5 minutes, then add minced garlic for 30 seconds.
Add the ground beef. Brown for 6–8 minutes, breaking it up, until no pink remains. Drain excess fat.
Stir in the drained black beans, drained corn, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Cook 2 more minutes to warm through.
Warm tortillas: 30 seconds in a dry skillet over medium, or wrap in foil and warm at 300°F for 5 minutes.
Assemble each burrito: tortilla, scoop of rice, scoop of beef-bean mixture, sprinkle of cheddar. Roll: tuck sides, fold bottom up, roll forward.

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Foil pan: beef + bean + corn filling. Gallon bag: rice frozen flat. Quart bag: shredded cheddar. Tortillas in original packaging.
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Bake filling pan covered at 375F 20-25 min. Spread rice in foil pan, bake alongside 375F 20 min. Warm tortillas. Assemble at the table.

title: Salmon Quinoa Power Bowls
description: Flaked salmon with edamame and spinach over quinoa with balsamic vinaigrette.

instructions.text: Thaw the salmon fillets if frozen — under cold running water, about 5–10 minutes.
Cook the quinoa: rinse 1½ cups, simmer with 3 cups water for 15 minutes, then rest 5 minutes covered. Fluff.
Heat oven to 400°F. Pat salmon dry, drizzle with a little olive oil and a pinch of salt. Bake on parchment 12–15 minutes until flaky.
Cook the edamame in the microwave per package (3–4 minutes); drain.
Thaw the frozen spinach (3–4 minutes in the microwave on defrost), then squeeze dry in a clean towel.
Whisk olive oil, balsamic vinegar, salt, and pepper for the vinaigrette.
Build the bowls: quinoa on the bottom, then flaked salmon, edamame, and spinach. Drizzle generously with vinaigrette.

ingredients.display_quantity: 1 lb (one bag), thawed and squeezed dry

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Foil pan 1: flaked salmon + edamame + squeezed spinach (layered, not mixed). Foil pan 2: quinoa. Small jar: balsamic vinaigrette (fridge).
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Bake quinoa pan covered at 350F 20 min. Bake salmon pan covered at 325F 15-18 min. Drizzle vinaigrette at the table.

title: Chicken Parm & Pasta
description: Just Bare chicken chunks with marinara, mozzarella, and parmesan over spaghetti.

instructions.text: Heat oven to 400°F (or air fryer to 400°F). Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
Bake the chicken chunks per package directions — usually 18–20 minutes in the oven, or 10–12 minutes in the air fryer.
Cook the spaghetti to al dente per package directions. Drain and toss with butter and a drizzle of olive oil to keep it loose.
Warm the marinara in a small saucepan over low heat — or skip and use it cold straight from the jar; it'll heat in the bake.
Transfer the cooked chicken to a 9×13 baking dish. Pour marinara over the top, then blanket with mozzarella and parmesan.
Bake uncovered 8–10 minutes until the cheese is bubbly and golden. Plate over the spaghetti.

_v1_legacy.storage_instructions: Foil pan 1: Just Bare chunks topped with Rao's marinara + mozzarella + parm. Foil pan 2: spaghetti with EVOO.
_v1_legacy.reheat_instructions: Thaw overnight. Bake chicken parm pan covered at 375F 25 min, then uncover 10 min until cheese is bubbly. Bake pasta pan covered at 350F 20 min. Serve chicken over pasta.
